fields.name: Journal Template Name
fields.tooltip: Contains the name of the relevant job journal template.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the journal line number.
The program fills in the field automatically and uses the number to keep track of the various lines in the job journal.

fields.name: Job No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting date you want to assign to each journal line. For more information, see Entering Dates and Times.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number for the journal line.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies an account type for project usage to be posted in the project journal. You can choose from the following options:

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the resource, item, or general ledger account to which this entry applies. You can change the description.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of units of the project journal's No. field, that is, either the resource, item, or G/L account number, that applies. If you later change the value in the No. field, the quantity does not change on the journal line.

fields.name: Direct Unit Cost (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost, in the local currency, of one unit of the selected item or resource.

fields.name: Unit Cost (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost, in LCY, of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: Total Cost (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total cost for this journal line. The amount is in the local currency.

fields.name: Unit Price (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the price, in LCY, of one unit of the item or resource. You can enter a price manually or have it entered according to the Price/Profit Calculation field on the related card.

fields.name: Total Price (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total price for the journal line. The amount is in the local currency.

fields.name: Resource Group No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the resource group code from the resource card if the line belongs to a resource.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a location code for an item.

fields.name: Chargeable
fields.tooltip: Indicates whether the usage you are posting for this job is chargeable or not.

fields.name: Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Contains the posting group assigned to the current entry. Depending on the account type in the Type field, the program fills in the field with the following information:

Type field
Posting Group field

Item

The inventory posting group


Resource

The field is left blank


G/L Account

This field is left blank

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Work Type Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which work type the resource applies to (when applicable).

fields.name: Customer Price Group
fields.tooltip:
Contains the customer if the customer to whom the job will be billed, the Bill-to Customer, is assigned to a certain customer price group.
The program uses the code when entering the unit price for the item on the line. The program checks the Sales Price table to see whether the customers in the price group for the code should pay a different price from the one that has been entered in the Unit Price field on the item card.

fields.name: Applies-to Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the project journal line has of type Item and the usage of the item will be applied to an already-posted item ledger entry. If this is the case, enter the entry number that the usage will be applied to.

fields.name: Entry Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entry type in the job journal as Usage if you enter job journals in the Job Journal window.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip:
Identifies the source of the entry.
The field is filled in automatically.

fields.name: Journal Batch Name
fields.tooltip:
Contains the name of the job journal batch associated with this entry.
To see the job batches that are available, choose the field.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the reason code that has been entered on the journal lines.
A reason code can be anything that indicates where an entry originated.
The program automatically retrieves the code from the Reason Code field in the Job Journal Batch table.
To see the reason codes in the Reason Code table, choose the field.

fields.name: Recurring Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the recurring method. The recurring method determines what happens to the quantity on the journal line after posting. For example, if you use the same quantity each time you post the line, you can reuse the same quantity after posting.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last date on which the recurring journal will be posted if you have indicated in the Recurring field of the project journal template that the journal should be a recurring journal.

fields.name: Recurring Frequency
fields.tooltip: Specifies a recurring frequency if you have indicated in the Recurring field in the project journal template that the journal is a recurring journal.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's or customer's trade type to link transactions made for this business partner with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of transaction that the document represents, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the transport method, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the address.

fields.name: Entry/Exit Point
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for either the port of entry at which the items passed into your country/region or the port of exit through which you shipped the items out of your country/region.
The code is used for reporting to INTRASTAT and will appear on the Intrastat Journal.
To see the entry/exit points that you can choose, choose the field.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related document was created.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number that refers to the customer's or vendor's numbering system.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for an area of the customer or vendor associated with this journal line.
The code is used for reporting to INTRASTAT and will appear on the Intrastat Journal.
To see the area codes that you can choose in the Area table, choose the field.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a code for the transaction specification that should be used in this journal line.
To see the transaction specification codes that have been set up in the Transaction Specification table, choose the field.
The code will be used in INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Serial No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Posting No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the number series that will be used to assign a document number to the journal line when it is posted. To see the number series that have been set up in the No. Series table, choose the field.
The program automatically copied the number series code from the Posting No. Series field on the journal batch. You can change the code.
If this field is blank when you post the line, the document number in the Document No. field will also be used on the posted line.

fields.name: Source Currency Code
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Source Currency Total Cost
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Source Currency Total Price
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Source Currency Line Amount
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Time Sheet No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a time sheet. A number is assigned to each time sheet when it is created. You cannot edit the number.

fields.name: Time Sheet Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number for a time sheet.

fields.name: Time Sheet Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that a time sheet is created.

fields.name: Job Task No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project task.

fields.name: Total Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total cost for the journal line. The total cost is calculated based on the project currency, which comes from the Currency Code field on the Project card.

fields.name: Unit Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the price of one unit of the item or resource. You can enter a price manually or have it entered according to the Price/Profit Calculation field on the related card.

fields.name: Line Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of planning line to create when a project ledger entry is posted. If the field is empty, no planning lines are created.

fields.name: Applies-from Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item ledger entry that the journal line costs have been applied from. This should be done when you reverse the usage of an item in a project and you want to return the item to inventory at the same cost as before it was used in the project.

fields.name: Job Posting Only
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Line Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line discount percentage.

fields.name: Line Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount amount that is granted for the item on the line.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the project's currency code that listed in the Currency Code field in the Project Card. You can only create a Project Journal using this currency code.

fields.name: Line Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that will be posted to the project ledger.

fields.name: Currency Factor
fields.tooltip:
Contains the current exchange rate between the job currency and the local currency.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

When Currency Factor changes, the values of the Unit Cost, Unit Cost (LCY), Unit Price, and Unit Price (LCY) fields are updated.

This field is used internally.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: Line Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount in the local currency that will be posted to the project ledger.

fields.name: Line Discount Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the amount of the discount that applies to the journal line.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

The values of the Line Discount %, Quantity, and Unit Price (LCY) fields are used to calculate the amount.

fields.name: Total Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total price in the project currency on the journal line.

fields.name: Cost Factor
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a cost factor if a cost factor was set up for the resource, the item, or the general ledger account on the journal line. The unit price is determined for the line using the following formula: Unit Cost * Cost Factor = Unit Price.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies information in addition to the description.

fields.name: Ledger Entry Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the entry type of the job ledger entry associated with the journal.

This field is used internally.

fields.name: Ledger Entry No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the entry number of the job ledger entry associated with the job journal.

This field is used internally.

fields.name: Job Planning Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the project planning line number that the usage should be linked to when the project journal is posted. You can only link to project planning lines that have the Apply Usage Link option enabled.

fields.name: Remaining Qty.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the resource or item that remains to complete a project. The remaining quantity is calculated as the difference between Quantity and Qty. Posted. You can modify this field to indicate the quantity you want to remain on the project planning line after you post usage.

fields.name: Remaining Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Shows the quantity of the items, resources, costs, or G/L account payments that remains to complete a job, expresssed in the base unit of measure.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin where the items are picked or put away.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of measure if the Type field contains Item and you have entered a quantity in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field in the Item Unit of Measure window, for this item. This field is automatically updated.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Quantity expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many base units of the item on the line have been reserved.

fields.name: Lot No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Price Calculation Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the method that will be used for price calculation in the item journal line.

fields.name: Cost Calculation Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the method that will be used for cost calculation in the item journal line.
